onboard.body: Map, Track, and Scan to see who businesses fund. Political contribution data. In your hands.
onboard.featureMap: MAP — Tap a business. See who it funds.
onboard.featureTrack: TRACK — Log what you avoid. See it on your scorecard.
onboard.featureScan: SCAN — Scan a product. See who's behind it and where their money goes.
onboard.featureScorecard: SCORECARD — Share what you did every week.
onboard.privacySubhead: Most apps collect everything. Here's what this one doesn't.
onboard.noSignInDesc: No accounts, no email, no user ID. Nothing to create, nothing to leak.
onboard.noLocationDesc: GPS is used once to center your map. Coordinates are never saved.
onboard.noVisitHistoryDesc: All your data — dates and counts — is stored on this device only. Never transmitted.
onboard.noServersDesc: Everything stays on this device. We only read public data.
onboard.openSourceDesc: Every line of code is public.
onboard.locWhy: Centers the map on businesses near you.
onboard.locPromise: Used once per session. Never stored.
onboard.notifWhy: Alerts for scorecard drops, reminders, and the things that matter.
onboard.notifPromise: Only the important stuff. Never spam.

infoContent.about.description: FCK puts political contribution data where it's useful — at the counter, in your browser, on your phone. Map a business. Scan a product. See where the money goes. Avoid what you want. Share what you did.
infoContent.about.organization: An open-source project. One person trying to make a difference by doing what they're good at.
infoContent.about.ethos: No accounts. No servers. No app store required. This app was built to work without permission — from anyone. All data is bundled. All code is public. If Apple or Google pulls it tomorrow, you can sideload it, fork it, or run the extension. The data lives on FEC.gov. The code lives on GitHub. Nothing depends on us staying online.
infoContent.transparency.data-source.title: Where does the data come from?
infoContent.transparency.data-source.body: Every figure comes from FEC.gov — the official U.S. government record of campaign finance. Every dollar amount in the app links to its original FEC filing. We don't editorialize. We show what's on file.
infoContent.transparency.fec-filings.title: Can I verify the numbers?
infoContent.transparency.fec-filings.body: Yes. Tap 'See full FEC record' on any business card. It opens the committee's page on FEC.gov. Verify any number yourself.
infoContent.transparency.confidence.title: How does matching work?
infoContent.transparency.confidence.body: Most businesses match to our bundled list automatically — high confidence, no badge. When a match comes from searching the FEC database, we show a MATCHED badge and a disclaimer. Tap the FEC link to verify. We never claim more certainty than the data supports.
infoContent.transparency.storage.title: What's stored on my device?
infoContent.transparency.storage.body: Only your avoidance actions: business IDs and dates you tapped Avoid, and platform avoids you logged. No times, no locations, no identifiers. The only outbound calls are to FEC.gov and GitHub — public data, one direction. Nothing about you is sent out.
infoContent.transparency.data-currency.title: How current is the data?
infoContent.transparency.data-currency.body: Contribution data follows FEC reporting cycles and covers every cycle since 2016. The entity list ships with the app and updates automatically when connected. Both are versioned and public on GitHub.
infoContent.transparency.limitations.title: What doesn't the app cover?
infoContent.transparency.limitations.body: V1 covers corporate PAC contributions (Schedule B). Individual executive donations (Schedule A) and independent expenditures (Schedule E) are planned for future versions. State-level campaign finance data is not included.
infoContent.faq.trust.q: Why should I trust this data?
infoContent.faq.trust.a: Every figure comes directly from FEC.gov. Uncertain matches are labeled. Every number links to its source. We never show data we can't cite.
infoContent.faq.wrong-match.q: What if a business is misidentified?
infoContent.faq.wrong-match.a: Matches from the FEC search always show a MATCHED badge and a 'verify before acting' disclaimer. Tap the FEC link to check. If something is wrong, let us know.
infoContent.faq.tracking.a: No. No accounts, no user IDs, no analytics. GPS is accessed only when you use the map and is never saved. The browser extension never stores which sites you visit.
infoContent.faq.partisan.a: This is a project about the outsized impact money has on politics. The app shows both R: and D: contributions for every entity. All data comes from FEC.gov. You see what's on file — you draw your own conclusions. We trust the data and we trust you.
infoContent.faq.report-error.q: How do I report an error or add a business?
infoContent.faq.report-error.a: See something wrong? Email [need to change!!] or open an issue on GitHub. The entity list is open source — anyone can submit a correction.
infoContent.faq.extension-sync.q: Does the browser extension sync with the app?
infoContent.faq.extension-sync.a: Not yet. Sync requires a backend, which we deliberately don't have. The extension has its own weekly summary. Sync is planned once a privacy-preserving approach is designed.
infoContent.faq.internet.a: The app was built to work offline — bundled data ships with every install as a privacy measure. An internet connection may be needed for live FEC lookups, barcode scanning, and entity list updates.
infoContent.faq.edit-platforms.q: How do I edit my platform list?
infoContent.faq.edit-platforms.a: Tap Edit at the top of the Track tab. Add or remove platforms anytime.
infoContent.faq.extension.a: Yes. Available for Chrome and Firefox. It flags businesses as you browse and lets you log avoids without opening the app. Same privacy rules — no browsing history stored, ever.

beta.resetButtonLabel: Reset app state for a fresh-install style test
beta.resetConfirmBody: This clears onboarding, launch flags, platform setup, avoid history, and barcode cache so you can test from the beginning again.
beta.resetDone: App state reset. Fully close and reopen the app to start from onboarding again.

scan.heading: SCAN A PRODUCT
scan.body: Scan it. Trace it. Here's the funding record.
scan.footnote: Works with UPC and EAN barcodes. Fill the frame and hold steady.
